item.text: I’ll add a regression that renders right-pane content with a multiline chat draft (`chat> first\nsecond`) and proves visual yank excludes the whole input block. Then I’ll change the prompt stripping to truncate from the last `chat> ` marker instead of checking only the final line.

items.text: Inspect terminal app test helpers for multiline draft coverage
Add failing multiline-prompt visual yank regression
Run required checks and real-agent verification

item.text: I’m checking existing `TerminalApp` test helpers and input sequences now; if there’s already a clean way to enter Shift+Enter or bracketed paste, I’ll use that instead of adding a lower-level-only test.

`TerminalApp` has a clean Shift+Enter path (`ESC [ 13 ; 2 u`) that appends a chat-buffer newline without sending. I’ll use that in a regression so the test covers the production app path the reviewer called out.

I’m adding the production-path regression to `tests/terminal_app.rs`: launch a chat, create a multiline draft with Shift+Enter, leave insert mode, run `Vky`, and assert only the transcript reply is yanked.
The multiline-draft regression is in place. I’m running it alone now to verify it fails before the filtering change.

item.text: The new test reproduces the reviewer’s case: the yank is `chat> first draft\nsecond draft\n`. I’m changing the helper to truncate from the last `chat> ` marker, which removes the full multiline input block.

I’m also updating the right-pane visibility splitter for the same multiline prompt shape. That keeps the chat prompt block treated as prompt content during rendering/caching, not as ordinary transcript history.
The prompt splitter now finds the last `chat> ` line and treats everything from there to the end as the prompt block. I’m rerunning the multiline regression and the harness prompt-exclusion test.

items.text: Inspect terminal app test helpers for multiline draft coverage
Add failing multiline-prompt visual yank regression
Run required checks and real-agent verification

item.text: I’m checking the prompt splitter line reference once so the final handoff points to the full fix, not only the selectable-line helper.
The prompt splitter is at `src/ui.rs:1828`; I have all final references now.

Fixed the multiline chat-draft edge case.

`TerminalUi` now removes the full right-pane input block from selectable/yankable lines by truncating from the last `chat> ` marker, so `chat> first draft\nsecond draft` is excluded as a unit. I also updated the right-pane prompt splitter so multiline drafts are treated as prompt content for visibility/caching. See [src/ui.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/ui.rs:1237), [src/ui.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/ui.rs:1645), and [src/ui.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/ui.rs:1828).

Added a production-path regression in [tests/terminal_app.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/tests/terminal_app.rs:420). It failed first with `chat> first draft\nsecond draft\n`, then passed after the fix.
